questions.question: Explain the differences between heavyweight and lightweight components in Swing. Why might mixing them cause problems?
keyPoints: Heavyweight components use native peer objects and map directly to OS widgets
Lightweight components are pure Java with no native peers
Heavyweight components always appear above lightweight in Z-order
Mixing causes visual glitches because of this Z-order issue
Solution: either use all lightweight or ensure proper layering

questions.question: Describe the structure of JRootPane in Swing. What are its four main components and their purposes?
keyPoints: Glass Pane: transparent overlay for event interception
Content Pane: holds visible GUI components
Optional Menu Bar: holds JMenuBar
Layered Pane: contains content pane and menu bar with Z-ordering

questions.question: What is the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) and why is it important in Swing programming?
keyPoints: EDT is the single thread that processes all GUI events
All Swing component operations must occur on EDT
Use SwingUtilities.invokeLater() for thread-safe updates
Long-running tasks block EDT causing frozen UI
SwingWorker provides background task execution pattern

questions.question: Explain the paintComponent() method vs paint() method in Swing. Which one should be overridden and why?
keyPoints: paint() is the top-level method calling paintComponent, paintBorder, paintChildren
paintComponent() specifically draws the component's content
Override paintComponent() for custom painting
Directly overriding paint() can break Swing's functionality
Never call paint methods directly; use repaint()

questions.question: What is Pluggable Look and Feel (PLAF) in Swing? How do you change the look and feel at runtime?
keyPoints: PLAF allows changing visual appearance without code changes
UIManager.setLookAndFeel() sets the L&F
Can use system L&F, cross-platform Metal, or third-party L&Fs
setDefaultLookAndFeelDecorations() controls window decorations
Changes apply to all components globally

questions.question: Explain the MVC architecture as implemented in Swing components. Give an example of how it works for JList.
keyPoints: Model stores data, View handles display, Controller handles input
Swing simplifies MVC by merging Controller into component
JList uses ListModel for data, custom cell renderer for view
Separation allows multiple views of same data
Changes in model trigger automatic UI updates

questions.question: What is double buffering in Swing and how does RepaintManager implement it?
keyPoints: Double buffering eliminates flicker by drawing off-screen first
RepaintManager manages all repaint requests
Creates off-screen buffer, draws to it, then copies to screen
Can be disabled via RepaintManager for debugging
Enabled by default in all JComponents

questions.question: Design a complete Swing application that displays a counter with increment/decrement buttons. Explain the thread-safety considerations and proper component architecture used.
keyPoints: Use EDT for all UI updates via SwingUtilities
Implement ActionListeners that update counter model
Model-View separation for data integrity
Use JFrame with proper content pane usage
Implement SwingWorker if counter involves heavy computation
Proper exception handling for L&F changes

questions.question: Explain the complete Swing component hierarchy from java.awt.Component to JButton. Also explain why JComponent was introduced and what additional features it provides.
keyPoints: Component → Container → JComponent → AbstractButton → JButton
JComponent adds: double buffering, borders, custom painting, key bindings, tooltips
JComponent provides unified API for all Swing components
It implements SwingConstants, Accessible, and UIResource interfaces
Supports Drag & Drop, and has built-in support for look and feel

questions.question: Discuss the painting mechanism in Swing. Explain the paint chain, double buffering, when to override paintComponent vs paint, and how to properly implement custom painting.
keyPoints: paint() calls: paintComponent → paintBorder → paintChildren
paintComponent is where custom drawing occurs
Always call super.paintComponent() first when overriding
RepaintManager handles double buffering automatically
Use Graphics2D for advanced rendering
Never call paint/repaint methods directly
